No Poverty.reason: The course content on intangible asset valuation does not directly address poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course focuses on financial valuation and intellectual property, with no connection to food security or agricultural practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly includes 'Real option valuation for pharma. R&D investment and licensing,' directly supporting Target 3.b on research and development for medicines.
evidence: Real option valuation for pharma. R&D investment and licensing

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly aims for students to 'understand and be able to identify social responsibility and sustainability issues,' directly supporting Target 4.7 on education for sustainable development. It also provides quality tertiary education.
evidence: help develop the student's ability to report on and analyze value of intangible assets
apply the concepts, methods, and standards to their real-world businesses

Gender Equality.reason: The course content does not address gender equality, discrimination, or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course focuses on financial and intellectual property valuation, with no connection to water, sanitation, or hygiene issues.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course content does not relate to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly supports economic productivity and entrepreneurship by teaching valuation for 'investment, IPOs, mergers and acquisitions, licensing, technology transfer,' aligning with Targets 8.2 and 8.3.
evidence: investment, IPOs, mergers and acquisitions, licensing, technology transfer
duty of care to manage and create value

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course title 'Reporting and Valuation of Intangible Assets' and department 'Technology, Innovation and Intellectual Property Management' directly address innovation and technological capabilities, including 'technology transfer,' strongly aligning with Targets 9.5 and 9.b.
evidence: Reporting and Valuation of Intangible Assets
Graduate Institute of Technology, Innovation and Intellectual Property Management

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's coverage of 'accounting pronouncements, and valuation standards' has a tenuous, indirect link to Target 10.5 on improving financial market regulation, which can contribute to reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course content does not address urban development, housing, transport, or community sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course explicitly states a core competence: 'Students will understand and be able to identify social responsibility and sustainability issues,' directly linking to Target 12.6 on integrating sustainability information into reporting.
evidence: 理解與辨別社會責任與永續議題 (Students will understand and be able to identify social responsibility and sustainability issues)

Climate Action.reason: The course content does not address climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience.

Life Below Water.reason: The course focuses on financial valuation and intellectual property, with no connection to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: The course content does not address terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly covers 'pertinent laws, accounting pronouncements, and valuation standards,' mentions 'litigation,' and emphasizes 'Respect Copyright,' directly supporting rule of law and strong, accountable institutions (Targets 16.3, 16.6).
evidence: pertinent laws, accounting pronouncements, and valuation standards

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's focus on 'technology transfer' and 'mergers and acquisitions' provides tools relevant to international cooperation and multi-stakeholder partnerships for technology dissemination and resource mobilization (Targets 17.7, 17.17).
